title: Lesson Quiz: What Is Economics?
description: Assessment of the concept that The study of choices, resource allocation, and economic systems.

choices.text: The study of how people use limited resources to meet needs and wants
choices.feedback: Correct. Economics studies choices people make when resources are limited.

choices.text: The study of how people avoid making choices
choices.feedback: Economics focuses on making choices, not avoiding them.

choices.text: The study of how only businesses earn profits
choices.feedback: Economics includes businesses, but it also includes individuals, governments, scarcity, and decision-making.

choices.text: The study of how resources are always unlimited
choices.feedback: Economics is based on the idea that resources are limited, not unlimited.

questions.prompt: Scarcity exists because people have limited wants.

choices.feedback: People often have many wants. Scarcity exists because resources are limited, not because wants are limited.
Correct. Scarcity exists because resources are limited while wants can be unlimited.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the best example of a need?

choices.feedback: Concert tickets are usually considered a want, not a need.
Designer shoes are generally a want because they are not required for basic survival.
A gaming system is a want because it is optional.
Correct. Food is a basic need because it supports survival and well-being.

questions.prompt: Land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship are the four productive resources.

choices.feedback: Correct. These are the four productive resources used in economics.
These are the correct four productive resources: land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes resource allocation?

choices.text: The process of making all goods free for everyone
choices.feedback: Resource allocation does not mean all goods are free.

choices.text: The way limited resources are distributed and used
choices.feedback: Correct. Resource allocation is the process of distributing and using limited resources.

choices.text: The idea that wants always disappear over time
choices.feedback: Resource allocation is about using limited resources, not about wants disappearing.

choices.text: The replacement of all human workers with machines
choices.feedback: This is not the meaning of resource allocation.
